headline: Warsh Hearing, Tesla Earnings: What to Watch Next Week
summary: Earnings season will kick into high gear as nearly 20% of the S&P 500 is scheduled to report their quarterly earnings. Big names reporting include Tesla, Boeing and Intel. Investors will also watch the scheduled testimony of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, for signals on how he’d lead the central bank.

raw.headline: Warsh Hearing, Tesla Earnings: What to Watch Next Week

headline: Earnings Outlook Remains Very Strong: A Closer Look
summary: We get into the heart of the Q1 earnings season this upcoming week, with 90 S&P 500 members on the docket. The line-up represents a cross-section of all sectors, ranging from blue-chip operators, consumer finance companies, and Tech players.

headline: Halliburton (HAL) Q1 Earnings on the Horizon: Analysts' Insights on Key Performance Measures
summary: Looking beyond Wall Street's top-and-bottom-line estimate forecasts for Halliburton (HAL), delve into some of its key metrics to gain a deeper insight into the company's potential performance for the quarter ended March 2026.

headline: Halliburton Q1 Earnings Preview: Here's What You Should Know
summary: HAL reports Q1 April 21; consensus sees 49 cents EPS on $5.3B revenues as drilling tech momentum meets completion headwinds.

headline: Iran Declares Strait Of Hormuz Open To All Vessels: Crude Plunges 14%, Airlines And Cruise Stocks Soar
summary: Iran's Foreign Minister declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial shipping. Crude oil plunged nearly 14%. Airlines and cruise lines surged.

raw.headline: Iran Declares Strait Of Hormuz Open To All Vessels: Crude Plunges 14%, Airlines And Cruise Stocks Soar
